intro: Margarocarpus cinerascens is a plant species, commonly known as Guaco or Rau, that belongs to the family Apocynaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health issues, such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Margarocarpus cinerascens include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which have been identified as the primary active compounds responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Margarocarpus cinerascens, such as teas and infusions, are commonly used to treat respiratory infections and coughs, and are also employed as a natural expectorant to relieve congestion.
